papers.title: Realization of a chiral photonic-crystal cavity with broken time-reversal symmetry
papers.abstract: Researchers realized the first truly chiral terahertz cavity with time-reversal-symmetry broken vacuum fields, with near-unity ellipticity at 0.66 THz and Q>50 under a 0.3 T field, offering a robust platform for chiral light–matter interactions.

papers.title: Combined biosynthesis and site-specific incorporation of phenylalanine derivatives from aryl aldehydes or carboxylic acids
papers.abstract: Genetic code expansion can be limited by the need to supply non-standard amino acids. The authors design bacteria that perform integrated steps of converting supplemented aldehydes to L-phenylalanine derivatives and incorporating them within proteins.

papers.title: Astrocyte activation in the ventrolateral medulla modulates breathing and arousal states
papers.abstract: The study shows that a subpopulation of brainstem astrocytes actively regulates breathing and brain states. Activating these cells alters respiratory patterns and increases sighing, revealing a key role in linking neural activity, arousal, and respiratory control.

papers.title: A polymer-coated nanowire sponge–based contact electrocatalytic system for simultaneous disinfection and removal of multiple micropollutants
papers.abstract: A polymer-coated nanowire sponge utilizes mechanical energy to produce reactive oxygen species, enabling rapid disinfection and removal of diverse pollutants from water without added chemicals or rare metals.

papers.title: Long-term comparative analysis of AAV9-mediated gene replacement therapies for spinal muscular atrophy in mice
papers.abstract: In mice, a next-generation gene therapy for spinal muscular atrophy improved survival and motor function more than a Zolgensma-like treatment, with fewer signs of liver and heart toxicity, supporting its promise as a safer, more effective approach.

papers.title: ZNF274 constrains lineage plasticity and drives intrinsic resistance to CDK7 inhibitors in pancreatic cancer
papers.abstract: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) classical and basal subtypes can interconvert. Here, the authors discover that ZNF274 loss drives a classical to basal transition in PDAC via suppression of ZEB1 and HERV-driven interferon signaling, increasing the sensitivity to CDK7 inhibition.

papers.title: Cryo-EM Structure of the TRPC1/5 Heteromer Enables Design of Antidepressant and Anxiolytic Drug with Reduced Side Effects
papers.abstract: Using cryo-EM, researchers solve the structure of the TRPC1/5 ion channel complex, revealing a unique drug-binding pocket. This enables design of JD03-02, a selective inhibitor with potential to treat anxiety and depression.

papers.title: PD-1 regulates latent effector differentiation of thymic cytotoxic CD8<sup>+</sup> T cells
papers.abstract: Programmed cell death receptor-1 (PD-1) has been implicated in thymic regulation of T cell development and function. Here, the authors characterize CD8⁺ T cell development in PD-1–deficient mice and show that PD-1 constrains the emergence of an effector-like program during thymic development, thereby shaping peripheral T cell responses and exhaustion in tumours.
